nodes.title: Track already-cloned objects in a map
nodes.why: This is the key to handling cycles and shared references correctly.
nodes.tradeoff: Uses extra memory, but avoids infinite loops and wrong identity.

nodes.title: Return primitives immediately

anchor.snippet: if (v === null || typeof v !== 'object') return v;

nodes.why: Primitives are immutable, so they do not need deep cloning.
nodes.alternative: Try to process every value the same way.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple fast path that keeps recursion focused on objects/arrays.

nodes.title: Reuse clone when object was seen before
nodes.why: If the same source object appears again, we return the same cloned object, preserving structure.
nodes.alternative: Always create a new clone each time.
nodes.tradeoff: One map lookup per object, but correct shared-reference behavior.

nodes.title: Create array or object based on source type
nodes.why: The clone must keep the same container shape as the original.
nodes.tradeoff: Tiny type check, but accurate output type.

nodes.title: Store clone in map before cloning children
nodes.why: This prevents infinite recursion for self-references like obj.self = obj.
nodes.alternative: Set cache after child cloning finishes.
nodes.tradeoff: Order is subtle, but this is the safe way for cyclic graphs.

nodes.title: Clone each property recursively
nodes.why: Each nested value is cloned with the same rules, so deep structures are handled consistently.
nodes.alternative: Only do a shallow copy and stop at one level.
nodes.tradeoff: More recursion work, but true deep clone behavior.
